idioms.definition: Having all modern conveniences and facilities (in a home or building)
examples: The new apartment comes with all mod cons – dishwasher, underfloor heating, and a smart home system.
They renovated the farmhouse and fitted it with all mod cons without losing its original charm.
The holiday cottage was advertised as having all mod cons, which made it very popular.
idioms.usageTip: 'Mod cons' is short for 'modern conveniences'. The phrase is especially common in property listings and descriptions of accommodation. It reassures buyers or renters that everyday comforts are included.

idioms.definition: Something ugly that spoils the appearance of an otherwise pleasant area
examples: The derelict factory has been a blot on the landscape for years.
Locals complained that the new power station was a blot on the landscape.
That abandoned building is a real blot on the landscape – the council should do something about it.
idioms.usageTip: Used to criticise something ugly in an otherwise attractive environment. Often used in planning, architecture, or environmental debates. Can also be used figuratively to describe anything that spoils an otherwise positive picture.

idioms.definition: The area surrounding a city from which many people travel to work daily
examples: House prices in the commuter belt have risen sharply as more people move out of the city.
They bought a family home in the commuter belt – close enough to London but with more space.
The new rail line was designed to improve connections across the commuter belt.
idioms.usageTip: A geographical and social term used in urban planning and housing discussions. The 'belt' refers to a ring-shaped zone. Associated with people who prefer suburban or rural living while still working in the city.

idioms.definition: An unpleasant urban environment with many large buildings and little or no greenery
examples: After years in the concrete jungle, she longed for the peace of the countryside.
The city centre was a concrete jungle with barely a tree in sight.
Growing up in a concrete jungle, he had never learned to appreciate nature until he moved away.
idioms.usageTip: Usually negative in tone, suggesting a cold, impersonal, and visually oppressive urban environment. The contrast with nature is central to the meaning. Can be used as a criticism of poorly planned urban development.

idioms.definition: A town near a large city that depends on the city for work and services
examples: Many satellite towns around the capital saw rapid population growth in the 1980s.
Living in a satellite town means a long commute but a quieter lifestyle.
The satellite town was originally built to house workers from the nearby industrial city.
idioms.usageTip: Used in urban planning and geography. A satellite town is distinct from a suburb because it has its own identity and infrastructure but relies on the larger city for employment and major services.

idioms.definition: To relax, especially by sitting or lying with your feet raised; to take a rest
examples: After a hectic week, all she wanted to do was put her feet up and watch a film.
You've been working non-stop – go home and put your feet up for the evening.
He finally put his feet up on retirement and spent his days gardening.
idioms.usageTip: An informal and friendly expression. Suggests physical rest and relaxation. Often used as advice or to describe a well-earned break. Pairs naturally with 'finally', 'at last', or after describing a period of hard work.

fillInExercises.sentence: The villa was fully equipped with ___ – pool, air conditioning, and a modern kitchen.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'All mod cons' means having all modern conveniences and facilities, typically used to describe well-equipped homes or holiday lets.

fillInExercises.sentence: Residents have complained that the new industrial complex is a complete ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'A blot on the landscape' describes something ugly that spoils the appearance of an otherwise pleasant environment.

fillInExercises.sentence: Property prices in the ___ have increased as families look for more affordable homes near the city.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'The commuter belt' is the zone surrounding a city where many people live but travel into the city for work.

fillInExercises.sentence: After ten years living in the ___, she craved open spaces and fresh air.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Concrete jungle' describes a dense urban environment dominated by buildings and lacking greenery.

fillInExercises.sentence: The town was originally a ___, built to house workers from the factories in the nearby city.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'A satellite town' is a town near a large city that depends on the city for employment and major services.

fillInExercises.sentence: You've been on your feet all day – why don't you sit down and ___?
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Put your feet up' means to relax and rest, especially by sitting down with your feet raised after a period of activity.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I've had enough of the [concrete jungle|concrete jungle]. I'm thinking of moving somewhere greener.

lines.speaker: Jess
lines.text: Where are you looking? Somewhere in the [commuter belt|commuter belt] so you can still get to work easily?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Exactly. I've been looking at a few places about forty minutes from the centre. One of them is a lovely [satellite town|satellite town] – it has its own market, schools, everything.

lines.speaker: Jess
lines.text: That sounds ideal. Is the property nice?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: It's a mid-terrace house, quite modern – [all mod cons|all mod cons], which is important for me. I want comfort.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: There's an old warehouse nearby that's a bit of a [blot on the landscape|blot on the landscape], but apparently it's being converted into flats.

lines.speaker: Jess
lines.text: Sounds like you've done your research. I hope you find the right place. You deserve to [put your feet up|put your feet up] after all those years in the city.

lines.speaker: Miriam
lines.text: We booked a cottage in the countryside for the long weekend. I cannot wait to [put my feet up|put your feet up] and do absolutely nothing.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: You've earned it. Is the cottage nice?

lines.speaker: Miriam
lines.text: Very – [all mod cons|all mod cons], a wood-burning stove, and a huge garden. Complete contrast to our flat in the [concrete jungle|concrete jungle].

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: That sounds wonderful. Whereabouts is it?

lines.speaker: Miriam
lines.text: It's just outside a small [satellite town|satellite town] about an hour and a half away. Very easy to drive to from the [commuter belt|commuter belt].

lines.speaker: Miriam
lines.text: There's a massive electricity pylon at the end of the lane – a real [blot on the landscape|blot on the landscape]. But honestly, I won't be looking at it. I'll be reading by the fire.

quizExercises.instructions: Match each idiom with its correct meaning.

pairs.meaning: having all modern conveniences and facilities
something ugly that spoils an otherwise pleasant area
the area surrounding a city from which people travel to work
an unpleasant urban environment with many buildings and no greenery
a town near a large city that depends on it for work and services
to relax, especially by sitting with your feet raised

quizExercises.sentence: After the long journey, we arrived at the hotel, checked in, and finally got to ___.
quizExercises.explanation: 'Put your feet up' means to relax and rest after a period of exertion or travel.

quizExercises.sentence: The listing said the flat came with ___, including a new kitchen, central heating, and a built-in washer-dryer.
quizExercises.explanation: 'All mod cons' (modern conveniences) is a standard phrase in property descriptions indicating a fully equipped, comfortable home.

quizExercises.question: Which description best fits a 'concrete jungle'?
options: A coastal town surrounded by forests, beaches, and open fields
A dense city district packed with office towers, car parks, and flyovers, with no parks or trees
A planned eco-city with green rooftops, cycle lanes, and urban gardens
quizExercises.explanation: 'Concrete jungle' describes a dense, unattractive urban environment dominated by large buildings and lacking natural greenery.

quizExercises.question: Which of the following is best described as 'a blot on the landscape'?
options: A newly planted woodland that has grown over a former industrial site
A crumbling, abandoned warehouse surrounded by a picturesque village
A modern eco-friendly visitor centre in a national park
quizExercises.explanation: 'A blot on the landscape' refers to something ugly that damages the visual appeal of an otherwise attractive area, like an eyesore building in a beautiful setting.
